text: After summarizing the earlier chapters, we move on to discuss how the GCC governments managed to establish a system for public higher education along with private sectors that increasingly provided more opportunities for citizens to access higher education. Increasing access to higher education has had positive effects not only on citizens but also on women, children of the expatriates working in the region and local children born to expatriate parents. As a result, women’s parents are now content with women studying at home. Children of expatriates who also grew up in the GCC countries can also graduate from universities in these countries.
explanation: The text presents generic, policy-level statements without concrete data or citations, a common characteristic of AI-generated summaries. It could be human-written as well, but the lack of specifics slightly favors AI authorship.
